translations.literal: Now some feudal lords command dozens of linked cities, with territories of a thousand li. If the court is lenient, they grow arrogant and dissolute; if the court is strict, they rely on their strength and form alliances to resist the capital.
translations.idiomatic: Now some feudal lords command dozens of linked cities and territories stretching a thousand li. If the court is lenient, they grow arrogant and dissolute; if the court presses them, they rely on their strength and form alliances to resist the capital.

sentences.zh: 今以法割削之，則逆節萌起，前日晁錯是也。

translations.literal: If we now try to reduce them by law, rebellion will sprout—the case of Chao Cuo in the past proves this.
translations.idiomatic: Should we now try to reduce them through legal measures, rebellious impulses will flare up—the fate of Chao Cuo in the past is proof enough.

sentences.zh: 今諸侯子弟或十數，而適嗣代立，餘雖骨肉，無尺寸地封，則仁孝之道不宣。

translations.literal: Now some feudal lords have ten or more sons, yet only the legitimate heir succeeds. The rest, though flesh and blood, receive not a foot of territory—thus the principles of benevolence and filial piety are not upheld.
translations.idiomatic: Now some feudal lords have ten or more sons, yet only the legitimate heir inherits. The rest, though they are flesh and blood of the same family, receive not a foot of territory—and so the principles of benevolence and filial piety go unfulfilled.

sentences.zh: 願陛下令諸侯得推恩分子弟，以地侯之。

translations.literal: I wish Your Majesty would decree that the feudal lords be permitted to extend their grace and divide territory among their sons, enfeoffing each with land.
translations.idiomatic: I beg Your Majesty to decree that the feudal lords be permitted to extend their grace and divide their lands among all their sons, enfeoffing each with his own domain.

sentences.zh: 彼人人喜得所願，上以德施，實分其國，不削而稍弱矣。」

translations.literal: Each of them would be delighted to receive what he desires. Above, it would appear as a bestowal of grace; in reality, it divides their kingdoms. Without any formal reduction, they would gradually weaken.'
translations.idiomatic: Every son would be delighted to receive his portion. From above, it would appear a generous bestowal of imperial grace; in reality, it would divide their kingdoms. Without a single decree of reduction, they would gradually weaken.'

sentences.zh: 於是上從其計。

translations.literal: The Emperor thereupon adopted his plan.
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor adopted his plan.

sentences.zh: 又說上曰：「茂陵初立，天下豪桀并兼之家，亂眾之民，皆可徙茂陵，內實京師，外銷姦猾，此所謂不誅而害除。」

translations.literal: He also advised the Emperor: 'The Maoling estate has just been established. The powerful families and land-engrossing houses throughout the empire, as well as the troublemakers among the common people, could all be relocated to Maoling. This would strengthen the capital region internally and eliminate cunning and lawlessness externally—what is called removing harm without resorting to execution.'
translations.idiomatic: He further advised the Emperor: 'The Maoling estate has just been established. The powerful families and the land-engrossing houses across the empire, along with the troublemakers among the common people, could all be relocated there. This would strengthen the capital region from within and eliminate subversive elements from without—removing the danger without shedding a drop of blood.'

sentences.zh: 上又從其計。

translations.literal: The Emperor again adopted his plan.
translations.idiomatic: Once again, the Emperor adopted his plan.

sentences.zh: 尊立衛皇后，及發燕王定國陰事，蓋偃有功焉。

translations.literal: In the elevation of Empress Wei and the exposure of the secret crimes of the King of Yan, Dingguo, Zhufu Yan played a meritorious role.
translations.idiomatic: In the elevation of Empress Wei and the exposure of the secret crimes of Liu Dingguo, King of Yan, Zhufu Yan had played a significant role.

sentences.zh: 大臣皆畏其口，賂遺累千金。

translations.literal: All the great ministers feared his sharp tongue and bribed him with gifts totaling thousands of pieces of gold.
translations.idiomatic: Every great minister feared the sharpness of his tongue and showered him with bribes totaling thousands in gold.

sentences.zh: 人或說偃曰：「太橫矣。」

translations.literal: Someone warned Zhufu Yan: 'You are being too arrogant.'
translations.idiomatic: Someone warned him: 'You are being far too overbearing.'

sentences.zh: 主父曰：「臣結發游學四十餘年，身不得遂，親不以為子，昆弟不收，賓客棄我，我阸日久矣。

translations.literal: Zhufu Yan replied: 'I have wandered as a scholar since I first bound my hair—over forty years. I never achieved success. My parents did not regard me as a son, my brothers refused to take me in, and my friends abandoned me. I have suffered this hardship for a very long time.
translations.idiomatic: Zhufu Yan replied: 'Since I first bound up my hair as a youth, I have wandered as a scholar for more than forty years. I never achieved anything. My own parents refused to acknowledge me as their son, my brothers turned their backs on me, and my friends abandoned me. I have endured this hardship for a very long time.

sentences.zh: 且丈夫生不五鼎食，死即五鼎烹耳。

translations.literal: Moreover, a real man—if he cannot eat from the five cauldrons in life, then let him be boiled in the five cauldrons in death.
translations.idiomatic: A real man—if he cannot dine from the five ceremonial cauldrons while alive, then let him be boiled in the five cauldrons when he dies.

sentences.zh: 吾日暮途遠，故倒行暴施之。」

translations.literal: My sun is setting and the road ahead is long, so I walk backward and act recklessly.'
translations.idiomatic: The sun is setting and the road ahead is long—so I act recklessly and without restraint.'

sentences.zh: 偃盛言朔方地肥饒，外阻河，蒙恬城之以逐匈奴，內省轉輸戍漕，廣中國，滅胡之本也。

translations.literal: Zhufu Yan enthusiastically argued that the land of Shuofang was fertile and rich, externally protected by the Yellow River. Meng Tian had once fortified it to drive out the Xiongnu. It would reduce the need for transporting supplies and garrisoning troops internally, expand the heartland, and serve as the foundation for defeating the Hu.
translations.idiomatic: Zhufu Yan argued enthusiastically that the land of Shuofang was fertile and abundant, shielded on the outside by the Yellow River—Meng Tian had once fortified it to drive out the Xiongnu. Holding it would reduce the burden of supply transport and frontier garrisons, expand the heartland, and lay the foundation for defeating the northern barbarians.

sentences.zh: 上覽其說，下公卿議，皆言不便。

translations.literal: The Emperor reviewed his proposal and sent it to the ministers for deliberation. They all said it was not advantageous.
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor reviewed his proposal and referred it to the ministers for deliberation. They unanimously declared it impractical.

sentences.zh: 公孫弘曰：「秦時常發三十萬眾筑北河，終不可就，已而棄之。」

translations.literal: Gongsun Hong said: 'In the Qin era, three hundred thousand men were regularly deployed to build the northern river defenses, but it could never be completed, and was eventually abandoned.'
translations.idiomatic: Gongsun Hong objected: 'In the Qin era, three hundred thousand men were deployed to build the northern river fortifications, yet the project could never be completed and was ultimately abandoned.'

sentences.zh: 主父偃盛言其便，上竟用主父計，立朔方郡。

translations.literal: Zhufu Yan argued forcefully for its advantages. In the end, the Emperor adopted Zhufu Yan's plan and established the Shuofang Commandery.
translations.idiomatic: But Zhufu Yan argued forcefully for its merits, and in the end the Emperor adopted his plan and established the Shuofang Commandery.

sentences.zh: 元朔二年，主父言齊王內淫佚行僻，上拜主父為齊相。

translations.literal: In the second year of the Yuanshuo era, Zhufu Yan reported that the King of Qi was privately licentious and of deviant conduct. The Emperor appointed Zhufu Yan as Chancellor of Qi.
translations.idiomatic: In the second year of the Yuanshuo era, Zhufu Yan reported that the King of Qi was privately dissolute and of perverse conduct. The Emperor appointed Zhufu Yan as the Chancellor of Qi.

sentences.zh: 至齊，遍召昆弟賓客，散五百金予之，數之曰：「始吾貧時，昆弟不我衣食，賓客不我內門；

translations.literal: Upon arriving in Qi, he summoned all his brothers and former acquaintances, distributed five hundred pieces of gold among them, and rebuked them, saying: 'When I was poor, my brothers would not clothe or feed me, and my friends would not let me through their doors;
translations.idiomatic: Upon arriving in Qi, he summoned all his brothers and former acquaintances, distributed five hundred pieces of gold among them, and rebuked them: 'When I was destitute, my brothers would not clothe or feed me, and my friends barred their doors to me;

sentences.zh: 今吾相齊，諸君迎我或千里。

translations.literal: now that I am Chancellor of Qi, some of you have come a thousand li to greet me.
translations.idiomatic: now that I am Chancellor of Qi, some of you have traveled a thousand li to welcome me.

sentences.zh: 吾與諸君絕矣，毋復入偃之門！」

translations.literal: I sever all ties with you—do not set foot in my door again!'
translations.idiomatic: I am severing all ties with you. Never set foot through my door again!'

sentences.zh: 乃使人以王與姊姦事動王，王以為終不得脫罪，恐效燕王論死，乃自殺。

translations.literal: He then sent someone to pressure the King with the matter of his incestuous affair with his sister. The King, believing he would never escape punishment and fearing he would be sentenced to death like the King of Yan, killed himself.
translations.idiomatic: He then sent an agent to confront the King with the scandal of his incestuous affair with his sister. The King, convinced he could never escape punishment and fearing he would be sentenced to death as the King of Yan had been, took his own life.

sentences.zh: 有司以聞。

translations.literal: The officials reported this to the throne.
translations.idiomatic: The officials duly reported the matter to the throne.

sentences.zh: 主父始為布衣時，嘗游燕、趙，及其貴，發燕事。

translations.literal: When Zhufu Yan was still a commoner, he had once traveled through Yan and Zhao. After he rose to prominence, he exposed the crimes of the King of Yan.
translations.idiomatic: When Zhufu Yan was still a commoner, he had traveled through the states of Yan and Zhao. After rising to prominence, he exposed the crimes of the King of Yan.

sentences.zh: 趙王恐其為國患，欲上書言其陰事，為偃居中，不敢發。

translations.literal: The King of Zhao feared that Zhufu Yan might become a threat to his own state and wished to submit a memorial revealing his secret crimes, but because Zhufu Yan held a position at court, he did not dare act.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Zhao, fearing that Zhufu Yan would become a threat to his own state, wished to submit a memorial exposing his secret transgressions. But with Zhufu Yan positioned at the heart of the court, he dared not act.

sentences.zh: 及為齊相，出關，即使人上書，告言主父偃受諸侯金，以故諸侯子弟多以得封者。

translations.literal: When Zhufu Yan was appointed Chancellor of Qi and left the passes, the King of Zhao immediately sent someone to submit a memorial, accusing Zhufu Yan of accepting gold from the feudal lords, and that because of this, many of the lords' sons had obtained fiefs.
translations.idiomatic: As soon as Zhufu Yan was appointed Chancellor of Qi and departed from the capital through the passes, the King of Zhao immediately dispatched someone to submit a memorial accusing Zhufu Yan of taking gold from the feudal lords, which was why so many of their sons had obtained fiefs.

sentences.zh: 及齊王自殺，上聞大怒，以為主父劫其王令自殺，乃徵下吏治。

translations.literal: When the King of Qi killed himself, the Emperor heard of it and was furious, believing that Zhufu Yan had coerced the King into suicide. He ordered Zhufu Yan arrested and turned over to the judicial officials for trial.
translations.idiomatic: When the King of Qi took his own life, the Emperor was furious upon hearing the news, convinced that Zhufu Yan had coerced the King into killing himself. He ordered Zhufu Yan arrested and handed over to the judicial officials for trial.

sentences.zh: 主父服受諸侯金，實不劫王令自殺。

translations.literal: Zhufu Yan confessed to accepting gold from the feudal lords, but maintained that he had not coerced the King into suicide.
translations.idiomatic: Zhufu Yan confessed to accepting gold from the feudal lords but maintained that he had not coerced the King into taking his own life.

sentences.zh: 上欲勿誅，是時公孫弘為御史大夫，乃言曰：「齊王自殺無後，國除為郡，入漢，主父偃本首惡，陛下不誅主父偃，無以謝天下。」

translations.literal: The Emperor was inclined not to execute him. At that time Gongsun Hong was serving as Imperial Secretary, and he spoke up: 'The King of Qi killed himself and left no heir. His kingdom has been abolished and converted into a commandery under Han. Zhufu Yan was the chief instigator. If Your Majesty does not execute Zhufu Yan, there will be no way to satisfy the expectations of the empire.'
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor was inclined to spare him. But at that time Gongsun Hong was serving as Imperial Secretary, and he spoke up: 'The King of Qi killed himself and left no heir. His kingdom has been abolished and absorbed as a commandery of the Han. Zhufu Yan was the root cause of it all. If Your Majesty does not execute Zhufu Yan, there will be no way to answer to the empire.'

sentences.zh: 乃遂族主父偃。

translations.literal: Thus Zhufu Yan's entire clan was executed.
translations.idiomatic: And so Zhufu Yan and his entire clan were put to death.

sentences.zh: 主父方貴幸時，賓客以千數，及其族死，無一人收者，唯獨洨孔車收葬之。

translations.literal: When Zhufu Yan was at the height of his favor, his guests numbered in the thousands. Yet when his clan was executed, not a single one came to claim the body—only Kong Che of Xiao collected and buried him.
translations.idiomatic: When Zhufu Yan was at the height of his favor, his guests and followers numbered in the thousands. Yet when his clan was executed, not a single person came to claim his body—only Kong Che of Xiao collected his remains and gave him a proper burial.

sentences.zh: 天子后聞之，以為孔車長者也。

translations.literal: The Emperor later heard of this and considered Kong Che to be a man of integrity.
translations.idiomatic: When the Emperor later learned of this, he regarded Kong Che as a man of true integrity.

sentences.zh: 太史公曰：公孫弘行義雖修，然亦遇時。
漢興八十餘年矣，上方鄉文學，招俊乂，以廣儒墨，弘為舉首。
主父偃當路，諸公皆譽之，及名敗身誅，士爭言其惡。
悲夫！
太皇太后詔大司徒大司空：「蓋聞治國之道，富民為始；
富民之要，在於節儉。
《孝經》曰『安上治民，莫善於禮』。
『禮，與奢也寧儉』。
昔者管仲相齊桓，霸諸侯，有九合一匡之功，而仲尼謂之不知禮，以其奢泰侈擬於君故也。
夏禹卑宮室，惡衣服，后聖不循。
由此言之，治之盛也，德優矣，莫高於儉。
儉化俗民，則尊卑之序得，而骨肉之恩親，爭訟之原息。
斯乃家給人足，刑錯之本也歟？
可不務哉！
夫三公者，百寮之率，萬民之表也。
未有樹直表而得曲影者也。
孔子不云乎，『子率而正，孰敢不正』。
『舉善而教不能則勸』。
維漢興以來，股肱宰臣身行儉約，輕財重義，較然著明，未有若故丞相平津侯公孫弘者也。
位在丞相而為布被，脫粟之飯，不過一肉。
故人所善賓客皆分奉祿以給之，無有所餘。
誠內自克約而外從制。
汲黯詰之，乃聞于朝，此可謂減於制度而可施行者也。
德優則行，否則止，與內奢泰而外為詭服以釣虛譽者殊科。
以病乞骸骨，孝武皇帝即制曰『賞有功，褒有德，善善惡惡，君宜知之。
其省思慮，存精神，輔以醫藥』。
賜告治病，牛酒雜帛。
居數月，有瘳，視事。
至元狩二年，竟以善終于相位。
夫知臣莫若君，此其效也。
弘子度嗣爵，后為山陽太守，坐法失侯。
夫表德章義，所以率俗厲化，聖王之制，不易之道也。
其賜弘後子孫之次當為後者爵關內侯，食邑三百戶，徵詣公車，上名尚書，朕親臨拜焉。」
班固稱曰：公孫弘、卜式、兒寬皆以鴻漸之翼困於燕雀，遠跡羊豕之閒，非遇其時，焉能致此位乎？
是時漢興六十餘載，海內乂安，府庫充實，而四夷未賓，制度多闕，上方欲用文武，求之如弗及。
始以蒲輪迎枚生，見主父而嘆息。
群臣慕向，異人并出。
卜式試於芻牧，弘羊擢於賈豎，衛青奮於奴仆，日磾出於降虜，斯亦曩時版筑飯牛之朋矣。
漢之得人，於茲為盛。
儒雅則公孫弘、董仲舒、兒寬，篤行則石建、石慶，質直則汲黯、卜式，推賢則韓安國、鄭當時，定令則趙禹、張湯，文章則司馬遷、相如，滑稽則東方朔、枚皋，應對則嚴助、朱買臣，歷數則唐都、落下閎，協律則李延年，運籌則桑弘羊，奉使則張騫、蘇武，將帥則衛青、霍去病，受遺則霍光、金日磾。
其餘不可勝紀。
是以興造功業，制度遺文，後世莫及。
孝宣承統，纂修洪業，亦講論六藝，招選茂異，而蕭望之、梁丘賀、夏侯勝、韋玄成、嚴彭祖、尹更始以儒術進，劉向、王褒以文章顯。
將相則張安世、趙充國、魏相、邴吉、于定國、杜延年，治民則黃霸、王成、龔遂、鄭弘、邵信臣、韓延壽、尹翁歸、趙廣漢之屬，皆有功跡見述於後。
累其名臣，亦其次也。
